title: pultenaea scabra var. biloba
intro: Pultenaea scabra var. biloba, commonly known as the bilobed bush pea, is a shrub native to eastern Australia. It belongs to the Fabaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The bilobed bush pea has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Australian bush medicine to treat a range of ailments.
